name: rls_policy_always_true
title: RLS Policy Always True
description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
detail: Table `public.admin_audit_logs` has an RLS policy `System can insert audit logs` for `INSERT` that allows unrestricted access (WITH CHECK clause is always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for -.

name: rls_policy_always_true
title: RLS Policy Always True
description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
detail: Table `public.notifications` has an RLS policy `Authenticated users create notifications` for `INSERT` that allows unrestricted access (WITH CHECK clause is always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for authenticated.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `avatars` has 2 broad SELECT policies on `storage.objects` (Avatar images are publicly accessible, avatars_public_read), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.

name: auth_rls_initplan
title: Auth RLS Initialization Plan
description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
detail: Table \`public.profiles\` has a row level security policy \`Users can view if allowed\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
detail: Table \`public.muted_conversations\` has a row level security policy \`Users can mute conversations\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
detail: Table \`public.project_applications\` has a row level security policy \`Project owners can update application status\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

name: multiple_permissive_policies
title: Multiple Permissive Policies
description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
detail: Table \`public.admin_audit_logs\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`anon\` for action \`INSERT\`. Policies include \`{"System can insert audit logs","System can insert audit logs via function",admin_audit_logs_write}\`

description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
detail: Table \`public.posts\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`anon\` for action \`SELECT\`. Policies include \`{"Admins can view all posts","Posts are publicly readable","Posts are viewable by everyone","Posts viewable - active for all, all for owner","Users can manage own posts"}\`

description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
detail: Table \`public.admin_settings\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`supabase_privileged_role\` for action \`UPDATE\`. Policies include \`{"Admins can write admin_settings",admin_settings_write}\`
